title: First Shots
teaches: the four-step combat sequence: hit roll, wound roll, save, damage.
intro: Your squad of Intercessor Space Marines has spotted a swarm of Tyranid Termagants across the field. They have just enough time for one volley of bolt rifle fire before the swarm closes. Watch each die carefully — every roll has a rule behind it, and we'll explain each one as it happens.

turns.narrate_before: Your Intercessors raise their bolt rifles. Each Marine fires twice — five Marines, two shots each, ten attacks total. The dice that follow will determine what happens.

outro: That was the full combat sequence: roll to HIT (3+ because the Marines' Ballistic Skill is 3), roll to WOUND (3+ because Strength 4 beats Toughness 3), roll to SAVE (Termagants need 6+ — their 5+ save is reduced by the bolt rifle's AP -1), then deal DAMAGE (1 per failed save, and Termagants have 1 wound each, so each failed save kills a model). Every shooting attack in 40k follows this exact sequence. Next scenario will introduce what changes when the defender is tougher.
